pi_items.source: Verified against setup_task.sh which explicitly seeds these target names into ~/Documents/lsb_targets.txt, and verifier.py which checks for them.
Verified against setup_task.sh instructions ('Exposure: 25 seconds') and verifier.py logic requiring abs(exptime - 25.0) < 0.5.
Verified against setup_task.sh instructions ('Capture exactly 4 frames') and verifier.py which scores based on count_light_frames >= 4.
Verified against setup_task.sh instructions ('Capture exactly 5 frames') and verifier.py which scores based on dark_count >= 5.
Verified against setup_task.sh directory creation and export_result.sh base directory scanning logic.
Verified against setup_task.sh instructions and export_result.sh which explicitly extracts this file path.
No explicit enforcement or textual appearance of 'Observational Astronomer' was found in the task configuration scripts or environment.
While setup_task.sh mentions 'Extragalactic Research Group', the exact industry string is not instantiated or checked.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates in a simulated environment using synthetic KStars CCD imagery. It explicitly requires capturing exactly 4 LIGHT frames per target at 25s exposure for real-world galaxies Malin 1, Malin 2, and UGC 1382. It also strictly requires capturing 5 DARK calibration frames at 25s exposure in /home/ga/Images/LSB_survey/calibration/darks_25s/ while disregarding 3 pre-seeded stale dark frames (stale_dark_001.fits to 003.fits) created during setup. A final report must be written to /home/ga/Documents/lsb_survey_report.txt containing the 3 target names.
